en: Homophones are words that have the same pronunciation but have different meanings or spellings.
ko: 동음이의어는 같은 발음이지만 의미나 철자가 다른 단어야.
ja: 同音異義語は発音が同じで意味やスペルが異なる単語だ
zhTW: 同音異義詞是發音相同但意義或拼寫不同的詞
vi: Tu dong am la nhung tu co cach phat am giong nhau nhung nghia hoac chinh ta khac nhau

en: Here are some examples. Eight. I ate a banana for a snack.
ja: 例を挙げよう。Ate。おやつにバナナを食べた
zhTW: 這裡有一些例子。Ate。我吃了一根香蕉當點心
vi: Day la mot so vi du. Ate. Toi an mot qua chuoi lam bua an nhe

en: Or the number eight. New could mean that you bought a new car.
ko: New는 new car를 샀다는 뜻일 수도 있고. 또는 새로
ja: または数字のeight。Newは新しい車を買ったとか
zhTW: 或是數字八。New可能意味著你買了一輛新車
vi: Hoac so tam. New co the la ban mua xe moi

en: Or you could be talking about something that you found out about.
ko: New는 new car를 샀다는 뜻일 수도 있고. 또는 새로
ja: 何かを知ったということを話している
zhTW: 或者你可能在談論你剛知道的事情
vi: Hoac ban co the dang noi ve dieu ban vua biet

en: Something that you now know. I knew about the problem you had at work.
ko: 알게 된 것일 수도 있어. 이제 아는 것. I knew about the problem you had at work.
ja: 今は知っていること。あなたの仕事の問題を知ってた
zhTW: 你現在知道的事。我知道你工作上的問題
vi: Dieu ban bay gio biet. Toi da biet ve van de cua ban o noi lam viec
